framework.name: NIST SP 800-161 Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
framework.description: NIST SP 800-161 Rev. 1 provides guidance to help organizations identify, assess, and respond to cybersecurity risks throughout the supply chain at all levels of the organization. It establishes a multi-tiered C-SCRM approach with three organizational levels, three maturity tiers of practices, and eight key practices for managing supplier and supply chain risk.
framework.verification_notes: Structure verified against NIST SP 800-161 Rev. 1 (May 2022, updated November 2024). Foundational/Sustaining/Enhancing practices from Chapter 3, Sections 3.4.1-3.4.3. Key Practices (KP.1-KP.8) from NIST IR 8276. Framework references NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 SR control family rather than defining its own control codes.

concepts.definition_en: The NIST C-SCRM framework organizes cybersecurity supply chain risk management into three organizational levels, three maturity tiers of practices, and eight key practices that apply across all levels.
concepts.definition_nb: The NIST C-SCRM framework organizes cybersecurity supply chain risk management into three organizational levels, three maturity tiers of practices, and eight key practices that apply across all levels.

concepts.definition_en: The three-tier organizational hierarchy for implementing C-SCRM, from strategic enterprise governance down to system-level implementation.
concepts.definition_nb: The three-tier organizational hierarchy for implementing C-SCRM, from strategic enterprise governance down to system-level implementation.

concepts.definition_en: The strategic level where executive leadership establishes C-SCRM governance, policies, roles, and the overall risk management strategy. Sets enterprise-wide C-SCRM objectives and resource allocations.
concepts.definition_nb: The strategic level where executive leadership establishes C-SCRM governance, policies, roles, and the overall risk management strategy. Sets enterprise-wide C-SCRM objectives and resource allocations.

concepts.definition_en: The operational level where enterprise C-SCRM strategy is translated into mission-specific policies, processes, and procedures. Bridges strategic direction with system-level implementation.
concepts.definition_nb: The operational level where enterprise C-SCRM strategy is translated into mission-specific policies, processes, and procedures. Bridges strategic direction with system-level implementation.

concepts.definition_en: The tactical level where C-SCRM controls are implemented, assessed, and monitored in specific systems, applications, services, and supply chain components.
concepts.definition_nb: The tactical level where C-SCRM controls are implemented, assessed, and monitored in specific systems, applications, services, and supply chain components.

concepts.definition_en: Three maturity tiers of C-SCRM practices from the foundational baseline through sustaining and enhancing levels. Organizations typically implement practices in sequence to build C-SCRM capability.
concepts.definition_nb: Three maturity tiers of C-SCRM practices from the foundational baseline through sustaining and enhancing levels. Organizations typically implement practices in sequence to build C-SCRM capability.

concepts.definition_en: Baseline actions for establishing a C-SCRM capability. Organizations should implement all foundational practices before progressing to sustaining or enhancing tiers.
concepts.definition_nb: Baseline actions for establishing a C-SCRM capability. Organizations should implement all foundational practices before progressing to sustaining or enhancing tiers.

concepts.definition_en: Obtain documented commitment from senior leadership to the C-SCRM program, including allocation of resources and designation of accountable roles for supply chain risk.
concepts.definition_nb: Obtain documented commitment from senior leadership to the C-SCRM program, including allocation of resources and designation of accountable roles for supply chain risk.

concepts.name_en: Implement Risk Management Hierarchy and Processes
concepts.definition_en: Establish a risk management hierarchy across the three organizational levels and implement repeatable processes for identifying, assessing, responding to, and monitoring supply chain risks.
concepts.definition_nb: Establish a risk management hierarchy across the three organizational levels and implement repeatable processes for identifying, assessing, responding to, and monitoring supply chain risks.

concepts.name_en: Measure Criticality of Suppliers, Products, and Services
concepts.name_nb: Mål kritikalitet for leverandører, produkter og tjenester
concepts.definition_en: Define and apply criticality criteria to prioritize C-SCRM investments on suppliers, components, and services that pose the greatest risk to mission or business operations.
concepts.definition_nb: Define and apply criticality criteria to prioritize C-SCRM investments on suppliers, components, and services that pose the greatest risk to mission or business operations.

concepts.definition_en: Embed C-SCRM requirements, evaluation criteria, and contract clauses into procurement and acquisition processes to ensure supply chain risk is addressed from the outset.
concepts.definition_nb: Embed C-SCRM requirements, evaluation criteria, and contract clauses into procurement and acquisition processes to ensure supply chain risk is addressed from the outset.

concepts.definition_en: Perform risk assessments of suppliers, vendors, and third-party service providers commensurate with the criticality of their products and services to the organization.
concepts.definition_nb: Perform risk assessments of suppliers, vendors, and third-party service providers commensurate with the criticality of their products and services to the organization.

concepts.name_en: Monitor Embedded Software and Firmware Components
concepts.definition_en: Identify and track software, firmware, and hardware components embedded in acquired systems and products, including maintaining a software bill of materials (SBOM) where applicable.
concepts.definition_nb: Identify and track software, firmware, and hardware components embedded in acquired systems and products, including maintaining a software bill of materials (SBOM) where applicable.

concepts.name_en: Deploy Quality Assurance and Control Processes
concepts.definition_en: Establish quality assurance and testing processes to verify that acquired components and systems meet security and functional requirements before deployment.
concepts.definition_nb: Establish quality assurance and testing processes to verify that acquired components and systems meet security and functional requirements before deployment.

concepts.definition_en: Implement internal audit and compliance review processes to verify that C-SCRM policies, procedures, and controls are being followed consistently across the organization.
concepts.definition_nb: Implement internal audit and compliance review processes to verify that C-SCRM policies, procedures, and controls are being followed consistently across the organization.

concepts.name_en: Create Supply Chain Incident Response Plans
concepts.definition_en: Develop and maintain incident response plans that specifically address supply chain compromise scenarios, including procedures for supplier notification, component quarantine, and recovery.
concepts.definition_nb: Develop and maintain incident response plans that specifically address supply chain compromise scenarios, including procedures for supplier notification, component quarantine, and recovery.

concepts.definition_en: Advanced practices for maturing and maintaining a C-SCRM capability. Build on foundational practices to increase rigor, continuity, and depth of supply chain risk management.
concepts.definition_nb: Advanced practices for maturing and maintaining a C-SCRM capability. Build on foundational practices to increase rigor, continuity, and depth of supply chain risk management.

concepts.definition_en: Assess suppliers using formal security certifications and accreditations (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2, FedRAMP) as evidence of security capability, supplementing internal assessments.
concepts.definition_nb: Assess suppliers using formal security certifications and accreditations (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2, FedRAMP) as evidence of security capability, supplementing internal assessments.

concepts.definition_en: Implement ongoing monitoring of critical suppliers' risk posture using threat intelligence, public reporting, and direct communication to detect changes that may affect supply chain risk.
concepts.definition_nb: Implement ongoing monitoring of critical suppliers' risk posture using threat intelligence, public reporting, and direct communication to detect changes that may affect supply chain risk.

concepts.name_en: Embed C-SCRM Requirements in Supplier Contracts
concepts.definition_en: Include specific, enforceable C-SCRM requirements in supplier contracts and service agreements, including rights to audit, incident notification obligations, and security baseline requirements.
concepts.definition_nb: Include specific, enforceable C-SCRM requirements in supplier contracts and service agreements, including rights to audit, incident notification obligations, and security baseline requirements.

concepts.name_en: Include Critical Suppliers in Incident Response
concepts.definition_en: Integrate critical suppliers into the organization's incident response plans and exercises, establishing clear communication protocols and coordinated response procedures for supply chain incidents.
concepts.definition_nb: Integrate critical suppliers into the organization's incident response plans and exercises, establishing clear communication protocols and coordinated response procedures for supply chain incidents.

concepts.name_en: Collaborate with Suppliers on Security Improvements
concepts.definition_en: Establish collaborative relationships with key suppliers to jointly identify vulnerabilities, share threat intelligence, and coordinate improvements to supply chain security practices.
concepts.definition_nb: Establish collaborative relationships with key suppliers to jointly identify vulnerabilities, share threat intelligence, and coordinate improvements to supply chain security practices.

concepts.definition_en: Define, collect, and analyze metrics to measure the effectiveness of the C-SCRM program, track risk trends, and support informed decision-making by senior leadership.
concepts.definition_nb: Define, collect, and analyze metrics to measure the effectiveness of the C-SCRM program, track risk trends, and support informed decision-making by senior leadership.

concepts.definition_en: Advanced automation and quantitative approaches for organizations with mature C-SCRM capabilities. Provide greater efficiency, precision, and analytical depth in supply chain risk management.
concepts.definition_nb: Advanced automation and quantitative approaches for organizations with mature C-SCRM capabilities. Provide greater efficiency, precision, and analytical depth in supply chain risk management.

concepts.definition_en: Leverage automation tools to streamline supply chain risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and reporting, reducing manual effort and improving consistency and speed of C-SCRM activities.
concepts.definition_nb: Leverage automation tools to streamline supply chain risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and reporting, reducing manual effort and improving consistency and speed of C-SCRM activities.

concepts.definition_en: Use probabilistic and quantitative risk analysis methods to more precisely measure supply chain risk exposure, enabling cost-benefit analysis of C-SCRM investments and risk transfer decisions.
concepts.definition_nb: Use probabilistic and quantitative risk analysis methods to more precisely measure supply chain risk exposure, enabling cost-benefit analysis of C-SCRM investments and risk transfer decisions.

concepts.definition_en: Eight key practices identified in NIST IR 8276 that are essential for effective C-SCRM across all organizational levels and maturity tiers.
concepts.definition_nb: Eight key practices identified in NIST IR 8276 that are essential for effective C-SCRM across all organizational levels and maturity tiers.

concepts.name_nb: Integrer C-SCRM på tvers av organisasjonen
concepts.definition_en: Embed C-SCRM into all relevant organizational functions, including procurement, IT, security, legal, and business units, to ensure comprehensive and consistent supply chain risk management.
concepts.definition_nb: Embed C-SCRM into all relevant organizational functions, including procurement, IT, security, legal, and business units, to ensure comprehensive and consistent supply chain risk management.

concepts.definition_en: Create a dedicated C-SCRM program with defined mission, governance structure, roles and responsibilities, budget, and authority to implement supply chain risk management across the organization.
concepts.definition_nb: Create a dedicated C-SCRM program with defined mission, governance structure, roles and responsibilities, budget, and authority to implement supply chain risk management across the organization.

concepts.definition_en: Identify suppliers of critical products and services, establish tiered management approaches based on criticality, and maintain current information on supplier security posture and relationships.
concepts.definition_nb: Identify suppliers of critical products and services, establish tiered management approaches based on criticality, and maintain current information on supplier security posture and relationships.

concepts.definition_en: Map and document the full supply chain for critical systems and services, including sub-tier suppliers, to identify dependencies, single points of failure, and potential risk entry points.
concepts.definition_nb: Map and document the full supply chain for critical systems and services, including sub-tier suppliers, to identify dependencies, single points of failure, and potential risk entry points.

concepts.definition_en: Build strong working relationships with strategically important suppliers through regular communication, information sharing, joint exercises, and mutual understanding of security expectations.
concepts.definition_nb: Build strong working relationships with strategically important suppliers through regular communication, information sharing, joint exercises, and mutual understanding of security expectations.

concepts.name_en: Include Key Suppliers in Resilience and Improvement Activities
concepts.name_nb: Inkluder nøkkelleverandører i resiliens- og forbedringsaktiviteter
concepts.definition_en: Involve critical suppliers in business continuity planning, disaster recovery exercises, lessons-learned reviews, and continuous improvement initiatives to strengthen the extended enterprise's resilience.
concepts.definition_nb: Involve critical suppliers in business continuity planning, disaster recovery exercises, lessons-learned reviews, and continuous improvement initiatives to strengthen the extended enterprise's resilience.

concepts.name_en: Assess and Monitor Throughout the Supplier Relationship
concepts.name_nb: Vurder og overvåk gjennom hele leverandørforholdet
concepts.definition_en: Conduct C-SCRM assessments at key points in the supplier lifecycle—pre-contract, during delivery, and at renewal—and maintain continuous risk monitoring throughout the relationship.
concepts.definition_nb: Conduct C-SCRM assessments at key points in the supplier lifecycle—pre-contract, during delivery, and at renewal—and maintain continuous risk monitoring throughout the relationship.

concepts.name_en: Plan for the Full Life Cycle
concepts.definition_en: Address C-SCRM from initial acquisition through ongoing operations to end-of-life disposal, including secure decommissioning, data sanitization, and management of legacy components.
concepts.definition_nb: Address C-SCRM from initial acquisition through ongoing operations to end-of-life disposal, including secure decommissioning, data sanitization, and management of legacy components.
